SoFP: Current vs non-current split for assets and liabilities where required (IAS 1).
Minimum line items and further sub-classification when relevant (e.g. PPE, inventories, receivables, cash, borrowings, tax, equity components).
Equity section: share capital, reserves (including retained earnings / revaluation where applicable), NCI if consolidated.
Subtotals: total assets; total liabilities; net assets / total equity; tie to accounting equation.
Present tax and deferred tax in line with syllabus treatment; do not mix operating creditor lines with borrowings.
Disclose accounting policies and judgements where the question requires (IAS 1 notes cross-reference).
SoPL: Revenue down to profit before tax in logical order; finance costs and investment income separated where material.
Tax expense line; profit for the period; attribute to owners and NCI if consolidated.
Other comprehensive income (before or below tax) when the scenario includes OCI items.
EPS line only when required by the question (IAS 33).
Avoid netting income and expenses unless standards permit; show by function or nature consistently.
SoCF: Three headings: operating, investing, financing (IAS 7) — direct or indirect method as the question states.
Operating: start from PBT or profit for period; adjust for non-cash and working capital; show interest/tax paid in operating unless question specifies otherwise.
Financing: equity/debt proceeds and repayments; dividends paid (often financing).
Cash and cash equivalents at start and end; reconcile to SoFP cash/bank/overdraft lines.
Disclose non-cash investing/financing if the question includes significant non-cash transactions.
Notes: Identify the standard (e.g. IAS 16, IFRS 15) and tie note headings to measurement, recognition, and required disclosures.
For PPE: classes, cost/model, depreciation, impairments, reconciling carrying amounts.
For provisions: opening, movements, closing; compare to SoFP line.
Cross-reference amounts to primary statements where examiners expect reconciliation.
